message:
chore(gitignore): ignore .rch-tmp/ scratch directory from remote_compilation_helper

`rch` (remote_compilation_helper) is the local-cargo wrapper that
offloads compilation to a remote build host. On every invocation it
materializes the artifact bundle it is about to ship at the repo
root under `.rch-tmp/`, populates it with the workspace inputs the
remote needs, and clears or rewrites it on the next run. Nothing
in `.rch-tmp/` is ever a source-of-truth — the contents are derived
from the workspace and exist only for the duration of one remote
invocation.

Until now this directory was untracked but un-ignored, so every
agent that ran `rch cargo test` would see `.rch-tmp/` show up in
`git status -s` and have to remember to skip it on every commit.
Two recent ru-workflow sweeps already had to filter it out
explicitly, and an unrelated `git add .` (e.g. from a future
inattentive agent or a skill that stages everything) would
accidentally commit hundreds of MB of derived workspace state.

Add the pattern under the existing `# Codex CLI cache` block,
which is the natural neighborhood for "agent-tooling scratch":

    # rch (remote_compilation_helper) per-invocation scratch directory —
    # holds the artifact bundle the local cargo wrapper ships to the
    # remote build worker; cleared on the next run.
    .rch-tmp/

Trailing slash anchors the rule to a directory so a hypothetical
`.rch-tmp` source file (extremely unlikely, but possible) is not
silently swallowed. Verified locally: `git status` no longer
surfaces the directory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -161,6 +161,11 @@ storage.sqlite3-wal.corrupt-*
 # Codex CLI cache
 .codex
 
+# rch (remote_compilation_helper) per-invocation scratch directory —
+# holds the artifact bundle the local cargo wrapper ships to the
+# remote build worker; cleared on the next run.
+.rch-tmp/
+
 # csplit scratch output at repo root
 /xx[0-9][0-9]
